2604.11806.title: Detecting Safety Violations Across Many Agent Traces
2604.11806.paper_summary: To identify safety violations, auditors often search over large sets of agent traces. This search is difficult because failures are often rare, complex, and sometimes even adversarially hidden and only detectable when multiple traces are analyzed together. These challenges arise in diverse settings such as misuse campaigns, covert sabotage, reward hacking, and prompt injection. Existing approaches struggle here for several reasons. Per-trace judges miss failures that only become visible across traces, naive agentic auditing does not scale to large trace collections, and fixed monitors are brittle to unanticipated behaviors. We introduce Meerkat, which combines clustering with agentic search to uncover violations specified in natural language. Through structured search and adaptive investigation of promising regions, Meerkat finds sparse failures without relying on seed scenarios, fixed workflows, or exhaustive enumeration. Across misuse, misalignment, and task gaming settings, Meerkat significantly improves detection of safety violations over baseline monitors, discovers widespread developer cheating on a top agent benchmark, and finds nearly 4x more examples of reward hacking on CyBench than previous audits.
2604.11806.paper_summary_zh: 為了識別安全違規行為，審計員經常需要搜尋大量的代理痕跡。這種搜尋很困難，因為故障通常是稀有的、複雜的，有時甚至是對抗性隱藏的，只有在分析多個痕跡時才能被檢測到。這些挑戰出現在各種情境中，例如濫用活動、隱蔽破壞、獎勵駭客和提示注入。現有的方法在這裡面臨幾個挑戰。每條痕跡的判斷者會錯過那些只有在多條痕跡中才能顯現的故障，天真的代理審計無法擴展到大型痕跡集合，而固定監控器對未預期的行為則很脆弱。我們引入了Meerkat，它將聚類與代理搜尋相結合，以揭示用自然語言指定的違規行為。通過結構化搜尋和對有前景區域的自適應調查，Meerkat在不依賴種子場景、固定工作流程或徹底列舉的情況下，發現稀疏的故障。在濫用、不對齊和任務遊戲的情境中，Meerkat顯著提高了安全違規行為的檢測，發現了在頂級代理基準上廣泛存在的開發者作弊，並在CyBench上發現了近4倍於之前審計的獎勵駭客示例。
2604.11806.author: Adam Stein et.al.
2604.11806.authors: Adam Stein, Davis Brown, Hamed Hassani, Mayur Naik, Eric Wong
